+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "lima-366-udp-ports-not-forwarded-to-host",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "lima: UDP ports not forwarded to host",
+    "description": "UDP ports not forwarded to host. Requested by 23+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current lima setup",
+        "description": "Verify your lima version and configuration:\n```bash\nlimactl --version\n```\nThis feature requires a working lima installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review lima configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant lima configuration:\nHi\n\nI'm running lima v0.7.2 and can't seem to get UDP ports to forward to the host as TCP ports are."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for UDP ports not forwarded to host",
+        "description": "> I know you'd brought up reassembling UDP datagrams, but TCP and UDP have the same max packet size (64 KiB), so I think forwarding the packets agnostically should actually just work as expected.\n\nIdk, I can't concentrate on this right now. You would probably have to set the MSS value in the TCP\n```yaml\nme@home ~ % lima nerdctl run -d \\\n    --name pihole \\\n    -p 53:53/tcp \\\n    -p 53:53/udp \\\n    -p 67:67/udp \\\n    -p 8080:80 \\\n    -e TZ=\"GMT\" \\\n    -v \"<base>/opt/pihole/etc-pihole/:/etc/pihole/\" \\\n    -v \"<base>/opt/pihole/etc-dnsmasq.d/:/etc/dnsmasq.d/\"\n    --dns=127.0.0.1 \\\n    --dns=1.1.1.1 \\\n    --restart=always \\\n    --hostname pi.hole \\\n    --cap-add=NET_ADMIN \\\n    -e VIRTUAL_HOST=\"pi.hole\" \\\n    -e PROXY_LOCATION=\"pi.hole\" \\\n    -e ServerIP=\"0.0.0.0\" \\\n    pihole/pihole:latest\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"UDP ports not forwarded to host\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> I know you'd brought up reassembling UDP datagrams, but TCP and UDP have the same max packet size (64 KiB), so I think forwarding the packets agnostically should actually just work as expected.\n\nIdk, I can't concentrate on this right now.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "me@home ~ % lima nerdctl run -d \\\n    --name pihole \\\n    -p 53:53/tcp \\\n    -p 53:53/udp \\\n    -p 67:67/udp \\\n    -p 8080:80 \\\n    -e TZ=\"GMT\" \\\n    -v \"<base>/opt/pihole/etc-pihole/:/etc/pihole/\" \\\n    -v \"<base>/opt/pihole/etc-dnsmasq.d/:/etc/dnsmasq.d/\"\n    --dns=127.0.0.1 \\\n    --dns=1.1.1.1 \\\n    --restart=always \\\n    --hostname pi.hole \\\n    --cap-add=NET_ADMIN \\\n    -e VIRTUAL_HOST=\"pi.hole\" \\\n    -e PROXY_LOCATION=\"pi.hole\" \\\n    -e ServerIP=\"0.0.0.0\" \\\n    pihole/pihole:latest"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
